title: Integrated methodology for correlating dynamic parameters with wheel wear progression in a scaled railway system
author: Sandoval-Valencia, Hurtado-Hurtado, et al.

steps.title: Simultaneously, 12 statistical features were extracted...
steps.instruction: Simultaneously, 12 statistical features were extracted from the synchronously acquired longitudinal acceleration signal (AceXTre_SisFebLab). The analysis revealed a significant correlation (>0.

steps.title: between the wear metrics and the...
steps.instruction: between the wear metrics and the vibration characteristics, with the mean vibration energy showing the strongest correlation at 0.

steps.title: This result confirms that the dynamic...
steps.instruction: This result confirms that the dynamic parameters captured using the proposed methodology are physically linked to the progression of surface damage, validating its suitability for direct correlative analysis.Fig. 3Example of the wear quantification algorithm applied to two different wheel states (820 and 2460 braking cycles).Fig 3

steps.title: Demonstration of correlative analysis
steps.instruction: Fig. 3 shows: (left) the original image with the Region of Interest (ROI) defined, (center) the extracted analysis area, and (right) the resulting wear mask with the percentage of damaged area calculated automatically.

description: The experiment had three phases: configuring sensors and designing tests, conducting controlled tests with synchronized data collection, and organizing data. This process creates a reproducible link between dynamic parameters and wear evolution.
